content:
## Deposit Timing Questions
Use the deposit cutoff when explaining whether a deposit request is still inside the current processing window. Northstar Bank’s deposit cutoff time is 17:00. If a customer asks whether a deposit made near closing will be counted today, anchor the answer on that cutoff and avoid promising availability beyond the published funds-availability rules. See "Transfer Cutoff And Delivery Policy" for transfer cutoff handling.

## Check Deposit Amounts
For check deposits, the first tranche amount is 275. When a customer asks why only part of a check appears available, explain that this tranche is the amount used for the initial availability treatment; do not imply that the entire check clears at once. A deposit is treated as large when it reaches the large-deposit threshold of 6725. Refer customers asking about timing beyond these amounts to "Funds Availability Policy".

## New Account Handling
A new account is identified using the account-age threshold of 30 days. When the account age is at or below that threshold, answer availability questions using the new-account treatment rather than the standard deposit explanation. If the customer asks for an exact release date and the date is not visible from the account record, ask a focused follow-up or route to the applicable availability procedure; do not calculate a release promise from these thresholds alone.
title: Deposit Availability FAQ
